title: Alon–Krivelevich problem on divisible subdivision bounds
statement_text: Improve the superexponential upper bound on $f(H, q)$ for subcubic graphs $H$ on $n$ vertices with $m$ edges, where $f(H,q)$ is the smallest integer such that every $K_f$-minor contains a $q$-divisible subdivision of $H$.
context_text: Alon and Krivelevich proved the existence of $f(H,q)$ for every subcubic graph $H$ and integer $q \geq 2$, but their proof yielded a bound of order $(q^2 n)^{q^3 n}$. The best known lower bound was $m(q-1)+n \leq f(H,q)$, leaving a vast gap. Alon and Krivelevich explicitly posed the problem of improving the upper bound.
paper_contribution: Proves $f(H,q) \leq 7mq + 8n + 14q$ for every subcubic $n$-vertex graph $H$ with $m$ edges and every integer $q \geq 2$, matching the lower bound up to a constant multiplicative factor and thus establishing $f(H,q) = \Theta(mq+n)$.
notes: Section 4 (concluding remarks and open questions) is absent from the extracted text; additional open problems stated there are not captured here.
